title: 1981-04-16_Pat_113.txt
key_claims: Gnosticism is essentially a profound "ontological psychology." The primordial crisis (the Fall) was an internal schism within the self, not just a spatial fall from heaven.
We experience the world as alien and hostile because we are alienated from our own missing halves. A partial self experiences a partial, broken world.
The "in-gathering of the scattered light" is actually the restored self rescuing itself. The saving Gnosis is intrinsic; the self already knows everything it needs once reunified.
Anamnesis (remembering) occurs when the partial self recognizes something familiar (a Platonic Form) in an alien landscape, abruptly proving it once belonged to a unified, total self.
recurring_concepts: **Salvator Salvandus**: The saved savior; the total intact self operating externally to rejoin its own severed internal parts.
reading_excerpt: Gnosticism is essentially a profound "ontological psychology." The primordial crisis (the Fall) was an internal schism within the self, not just a spatial fall from heaven.
